title: Migrate Stage-1 ASCR to Lumina-DiMOO (default high quality) + fair cross-model comparison

judge.rubric: strict prompt-following single-image clean judge, PASS when score>=0.5 (SAME rubric as Phase 11)

model.name: Alpha-VLLM/Lumina-DiMOO
model.type: unified discrete-diffusion omni model (~8B, LLaDA-based masked diffusion)
model.note: UniGenBench: ranked #1 among open-source unified models (Tencent Hunyuan leaderboard).

generation_settings.note: Lumina documented default high-quality settings (64 steps / cfg 4.0).

selector: Qwen3.5-9B (qwen_vl) original coarse ASCR (4x4 -> dilation -> project to 64x64 token mask -> reopen), same selector as the Phase-11 Show-o / MMaDA arms. Two-process file-IPC (Lumina .venv-lumina + Qwen .venv-qwen36).
reopen: native masked-diffusion inpainting: keep baseline VQ codes in memory, set selected token cells back to MASK, re-diffuse only those positions (no VQ encode/decode round-trip). Verified on GPU: changed_inside>0, changed_outside=0.
conclusions: Under the SAME clean Gemini rubric on Hard64, base-model ordering is Lumina-DiMOO 82.8% > Show-o 73.4% > MMaDA-8B 57.8%; +Qwen-9B coarse ASCR ordering is Lumina 84.4% > Show-o 78.1% > MMaDA 56.2%.
Lumina-DiMOO decisively beats Show-o under debiased pairwise: baseline 21 wins / 4 / 39 ties (84.0% decisive); +ASCR 18 / 4 / 42 (81.8% decisive).

Coarse ASCR + Qwen-9B never regresses Lumina (debiased 1/0/63) and adds +1.6pp clean; only 9/64 prompts were revised because Lumina's near-SOTA baseline leaves little compositional headroom for the selector to flag.

Trend across the three base models: the stronger the base generator, the smaller the ASCR headroom (Show-o +4.7pp / MMaDA never-regress / Lumina +1.6pp), but ASCR with the external Qwen-9B selector never hurts final quality on any base.
